title: ds-viz-choropleth-svg - option matrix (dark)
description: Test bench for splunk.choropleth.svg. Verifies all 5 documented options (svg, areaIds, areaValues, areaColors, backgroundColor). The SVG is a simple 5-region 'data center floor plan' with path IDs RACK-A..RACK-E; the data source binds each rack to a numeric metric.

options.markdown:
# `splunk.choropleth.svg` - option matrix

The SVG choropleth fills regions of an arbitrary SVG image with colour driven by a metric. Use it for **floor plans**, **building schematics**, **device topology**, or any bespoke geometry that has no Leaflet basemap.

**All options from the 10.4 PDF (5 total):**

- `svg`* (required) - literal SVG markup string OR an `svg+xml` data URI. Web URLs are documented as **not supported** per the prerequisites, though the PDF's multiFormat() example uses one. Stick to literal / data-URI.
- `areaIds` - DOS string binding the column whose values match `path id=` attributes in the SVG.
- `areaValues` - DOS string binding the numeric column used for fill colour.
- `areaColors` - DOS string for the fill colour mapping (typically `rangeValue` / `gradient` / `multiFormat`).
- `backgroundColor` - panel background behind the SVG.

**Data shape:** the search must return a string column matching path IDs and a numeric column for the metric. In this dashboard the SVG has 5 regions (`RACK-A` ... `RACK-E`) and the data source binds them to a `load` value 0-100.

viz_default_colors.title: 1. Default colours (rangeValue palette)
viz_default_colors.description: areaIds + areaValues only. Default areaColors uses the built-in rangeValue palette. Verifies the minimal contract.

options.svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 600 300"><rect x="0" y="0" width="600" height="300" fill="#1A2440"/><text x="300" y="24" text-anchor="middle" fill="#E8E8E8" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="14">Datacenter Floor</text><path id="RACK-A" d="M40,60 h100 v180 h-100 z" fill="#888"/><path id="RACK-B" d="M160,60 h100 v180 h-100 z" fill="#888"/><path id="RACK-C" d="M280,60 h100 v180 h-100 z" fill="#888"/><path id="RACK-D" d="M400,60 h100 v180 h-100 z" fill="#888"/><path id="RACK-E" d="M520,60 h60 v180 h-60 z" fill="#888"/><text x="90" y="160" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="14">A</text><text x="210" y="160" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="14">B</text><text x="330" y="160" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="14">C</text><text x="450" y="160" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="14">D</text><text x="550" y="160" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="14">E</text></svg>

viz_range_colors.title: 2. Custom areaColors via rangeValue(thresholdConfig)
viz_range_colors.description: Explicit threshold palette: green / yellow / red driven by load value.

options.svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 600 300"><rect x="0" y="0" width="600" height="300" fill="#1A2440"/><path id="RACK-A" d="M40,60 h100 v180 h-100 z" fill="#888"/><path id="RACK-B" d="M160,60 h100 v180 h-100 z" fill="#888"/><path id="RACK-C" d="M280,60 h100 v180 h-100 z" fill="#888"/><path id="RACK-D" d="M400,60 h100 v180 h-100 z" fill="#888"/><path id="RACK-E" d="M520,60 h60 v180 h-60 z" fill="#888"/></svg>

viz_gradient_colors.title: 3. areaColors via gradient(...)
viz_gradient_colors.description: Smooth two-stop gradient between low and high. Useful for heatmap-style visualizations.

options.svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 600 300"><rect x="0" y="0" width="600" height="300" fill="#1A2440"/><path id="RACK-A" d="M40,60 h100 v180 h-100 z" fill="#888"/><path id="RACK-B" d="M160,60 h100 v180 h-100 z" fill="#888"/><path id="RACK-C" d="M280,60 h100 v180 h-100 z" fill="#888"/><path id="RACK-D" d="M400,60 h100 v180 h-100 z" fill="#888"/><path id="RACK-E" d="M520,60 h60 v180 h-60 z" fill="#888"/></svg>

viz_background.title: 4. backgroundColor override
viz_background.description: Custom backgroundColor visible behind the SVG. The SVG itself has no opaque rect so the panel BG bleeds through.

options.svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 600 300"><path id="RACK-A" d="M40,60 h100 v180 h-100 z"/><path id="RACK-B" d="M160,60 h100 v180 h-100 z"/><path id="RACK-C" d="M280,60 h100 v180 h-100 z"/><path id="RACK-D" d="M400,60 h100 v180 h-100 z"/><path id="RACK-E" d="M520,60 h60 v180 h-60 z"/></svg>
